Home / Services / Pediatric Eye Care

Pediatric Eye Care

Vision development expertise for Richmond's children.

From newborns to college applicants, we provide age-appropriate eye care that meets Virginia's specific needs. Our pediatric exams screen for conditions common in Richmond children, including:

- High myopia from increased device use in Chesterfield County schools
- Sports-related eye injuries from youth leagues
- Allergic conjunctivitis exacerbated by local flora

We use child-friendly techniques in our Carytown office, complete with interactive vision games. Understanding that 80% of learning is visual, we coordinate closely with Richmond Public Schools to catch vision problems early. Our team includes Spanish-speaking staff to serve our diverse community.

Summer is the ideal time for back-to-school eye exams - we offer Saturday appointments to accommodate busy families.

Our Process

  1. 01 Step 1: Developmental vision screening
  2. 02 Step 2: Binocular vision assessment
  3. 03 Step 3: Digital eye strain evaluation
  4. 04 Step 4: School-specific recommendations

Benefits

  • Early detection of learning-related vision issues
  • Sports vision training available
  • Accepts all Virginia Medicaid plans
  • Bilingual staff available

Frequently Asked Questions

When should my child have their first eye exam?

The Virginia Department of Health recommends exams at 6 months, 3 years, and before kindergarten - earlier if you notice crossed eyes or developmental delays.

Book an Appointment

Ready to get started with pediatric eye care? Contact us today.

Schedule Now Or call: 555-opt-r94
